detail:
Start with ~40 expansion sources:
• 20 academic seeds (T1, see Corpus Registry)
• 8 grey literature anchors (IEEE ICICLE BoK, LEVI Toolkit, How People Learn, Saxberg essay, IEEE Press textbook chapters, ICICLE resources page)
• 6 review anchors: comprehensive recent surveys of ITS, EDM, LA, LE process, simulation, expert elicitation
• 6 from Programs & People Registry (LENS, CMU OLI, ASSISTments)

For academic literature: run one-hop expansion via OpenAlex.
For non-paper resources: harvest IEEE ICICLE resources page manually.
notes: IEEE ICICLE resources page is mandatory manual harvest. Categorize every item by content_type and primary_topic before pipeline runs.

detail:
Manually scrape and categorize https://sagroups.ieee.org/icicle/resources/
For each item:
• Assign content_type (AP/SG/PC/PP/CE/TP/GL/CO)
• Assign primary_topic from Topic Map
• Add to Corpus Registry or Programs & People Registry
• Flag as ieee_icicle_listed = YES

Expected: 50–100 resources on ICICLE page.
notes: This is the field's own curated list. All items are Tier 4 candidates at minimum. Complete before running algorithmic pipeline.

detail:
Run forward (citing) + backward (cited) expansion from all academic seeds via OpenAlex.
Endpoints:
• Forward: api.openalex.org/works?filter=cites:{id}
• Backward: referenced_works field

Expected raw output: 3,000–8,000 papers. Deduplicate by OpenAlex Work ID or DOI.
notes: LE field is smaller than biomedical — expect fewer candidates than MS pipeline. Supplement with conference proceedings (Learning@Scale, EDM, AIED, ITS, LAK).

detail:
For each candidate, compute:
  cross_seed_score = count of T1 seeds in whose 1-hop neighborhood this paper appears

Threshold: cross_seed_score >= 3 advances to Tier 2.
For conference proceedings not in OpenAlex: use Semantic Scholar API as fallback.
notes: LE has conference-heavy publication norms. Many important papers are at Learning@Scale, EDM, AIED — ensure these are indexed. May need to seed directly from conference proceedings.

detail:
For each topic T00–T17:
  subdomain_score = max(citation_percentile_in_topic, velocity_percentile_in_topic)
  velocity = citations_last_2_years / paper_age

Threshold: subdomain_score >= 0.70 within qualifying topic advances to Tier 3.
Normalize within topic, not across field (LE has very different citation norms across subfields).
notes: T07 (AI/LLMs) will have high velocity — apply extra scrutiny for conceptual vs. empirical contributions. T00/T16 (field definition) papers accumulate slowly but are foundational — lower velocity threshold to 0.60 for these topics.

detail:
Mandatory manual additions regardless of algorithmic score:
• All items from IEEE ICICLE resources page (Step 1)
• LENS @ JHU program materials and affiliated publications
• Conference best papers from Learning@Scale, EDM, AIED, ITS (last 5 years)
• IEEE ICICLE working group outputs and standards
• Non-English LE literature (Japanese adaptive learning; European vocational training research)
• Practitioner-generated resources (industry white papers, professional guides)
• ExpertTrace / CIRCUIT / MERIT program publications (APL-affiliated)
notes: Tier 4 should be ~20% of corpus given policy/grey lit importance in LE. Every T4 item must have documented selection_source.

detail:
For non-paper resources:
• Verify all entries in Programs & People Registry have url, primary_topic, description
• Cross-reference IEEE ICICLE community page for missing people/programs
• Add major conference series (Learning@Scale, EDM, AIED, ITS, LAK, ICICLE)
• Verify LENS @ JHU details are current and accurate
• Add any JHU APL affiliated programs (CIRCUIT, MERIT, PISTA) as PC/CO entries
notes: People and programs are first-class entities in this corpus. Knowledge graph edges depend on accurate registry.

detail:
Review full corpus against:
1. Topic balance: each T00–T17 approaches target_n from Topic Map
2. Content type balance: mix of AP/SG/GL/PP/PC/CE/TP/CO
3. ICICLE coverage: all ICICLE resources page items accounted for
4. LENS @ JHU: program materials fully represented
5. Recency balance: both foundational anchors and current work per topic
6. Practitioner vs. researcher voice balance

Set status = APPROVED or REJECTED for each entry with documented reason.
notes: QA metric: for each review anchor, compute overlap_pct = anchor_refs_in_corpus / total_anchor_refs. Target: >65% (lower threshold than biomedical due to conference norms).
